hypothesis: Incorporating DFT+U corrections specifically parameterized for 3d transition metal MOFs as an intermediate-fidelity level within a multi-fidelity ∆-learning cascade (GFN1-xTB → DFT+U → HSE06) will reduce band gap prediction error for transition-metal-containing MOFs by more than 50% compared to the two-level GFN1-xTB/HSE06 ∆-learning model, because the primary source of residual error in the current framework is the failure of GFN1-xTB to capture strong d-electron correlation effects that DFT+U can partially recover at modest computational cost.
